title: Kotlin vs Java
description: A quick look at why Kotlin is loved by developers.

slides.title: The Evolution 🦋
slides.content:
## Why switch from Java?

Java has been around since 1995! While incredibly powerful, Java can sometimes feel overly verbose—meaning it takes a lot of typing to achieve simple things.

Kotlin was designed to be concise. Less code means fewer bugs and faster development.

slides.title: Java Example ☕
slides.content:
Look at this simple "Hello World" program written in Java. Notice the extra boilerplate code (the class declaration, the long `System.out.println`).

Also notice the strictly required semicolon `;` at the end.

slides.title: Kotlin Example 🚀
slides.content:
Now look at the exact same program in Kotlin.
It's much shorter, cleaner, and you **don't even need semicolons** `;` at the end of lines! 🎉

quizData.question: Which of the following does Kotlin NOT require at the end of a statement, unlike Java?
quizData.explanation: Kotlin removes the mandatory requirement for semicolons, making the code cleaner.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Semicolons in Kotlin

You *can* use semicolons in Kotlin if you place multiple statements on the exact same line, but it is highly discouraged. Code is meant to be read, so stack it vertically!
